mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. A sustained month-over-month decrease in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.61% as of March 2008 reflects recent Fed rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 5 trading days as equity investors anticipate earnings compression from demand destruction.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% in March 2008, elevated relative to 2007 baseline
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales and housing starts jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously over a 2-month period, equity investors reduce growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand-side recession risk that neither series signals independently.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1065k units in February 2008, down sharply from 2006 peak
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it signals supply-side weakness and margin compression. S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 60 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades from reduced production efficiency.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.91 in February 2008, showing early signs of contraction
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth is a leading indicator of consumer income and spending. When PAYEMS declines month-over-month, it signals labor market weakness that precedes demand destruction. S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 20 trading days as equity investors anticipate lower corporate revenues.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,846k in March 2008, showing early employment weakness
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL interacts with monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) to determine real interest rates. When inflation rises while FEDFUNDS remains low or declining, real rates compress, but if inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is already near zero, stagflation risk emerges. The interaction of rising CPI with low FEDFUNDS produces negative equity returns within 20 trading days in late-cycle regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.57 in February 2008, FEDFUNDS at 2.61% in March 2008
regime_hint: stagflation risk regime with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of construction demand and consumer wealth effects. When HOUST falls below a 2-year moving average by more than 15%, it signals housing market collapse that destroys consumer balance sheets. S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 60 trading days as equity investors price in wealth destruction and reduced consumer spending.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1065k units in February 2008, down from 2.1M+ in 2006
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic demand. When quarterly GDPC1 growth decelerates to below 1.5% annualized, it signals late-cycle weakness. S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 60 trading days as investors anticipate recession and earnings downgrades across all sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11675.7B (Q4 2007), showing deceleration from 2007 growth
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
